# Codex Chef

Codex Chef is an unofficial, Windows-first Codex setup kit. It packages
reviewed global Codex guidance, conservative config templates, specialist
subagents, curated skills, MCP defaults, a local plugin, dry-run installers, and
validation gates.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/ucsahinn/codex-chef

Primary human entry points:

- [README](README.md)
- [Turkish README](README.tr.md)
- [Install guide](docs/install.md)
- [Verification guide](docs/verification.md)
- [Security model](docs/security-model.md)
- [Skills and agents](docs/skills-and-agents.md)
- [Knowledge base](kb/README.md)
- [Workflow surface map](docs/workflow-surface-map.md)
- [ECC compatibility](docs/ecc-compatibility.md)
- [Completion audit](docs/completion-audit.md)
- [Release notes](docs/release-notes.md)

## What It Installs

The reviewed install surface is described by `manifests/install-plan.json` and
previewed by:

```bash
node scripts/plan-install.mjs --all --json --redact-paths
```

The default managed Codex targets are:

- `~/.codex/AGENTS.md`
- `~/.codex/config.toml`
- `~/.codex/agents/*.toml`
- `~/.codex/rules/default.rules`
- `~/.codex/plugins/codex-chef-workflows`
- `~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json`

Optional global Git guards are not part of the silent default. They require the
explicit `-InstallGitGuards` or `--install-git-guards` flag.

## Core Capabilities

- 21 specialist Codex subagent role definitions.
- 3 bundled local plugin skills:
  - `codex-chef-operator`
  - `context-budget-planner`
  - `offline-diagram-triplet`
- 16 reviewed installable global skills in `catalog/skills.json` and
  `catalog/skills-lock.json`.
- Conservative MCP defaults in `catalog/mcp-servers.json` and
  `templates/codex/config.*.toml`.
- Bilingual task-focused knowledge base under `kb/`.
- No-write installer previews for PowerShell and Bash.
- Repo-only doctor command:

```bash
npm run codex:doctor
```

## Safe Defaults

Do not assume this repo enables external accounts, production systems, broad
filesystem access, deployment tokens, GitHub publishing, or browser session
imports by default. Authenticated and high-risk connectors stay disabled until a
task explicitly needs them and the user approves the access.

The installer does not commit, push, create releases, deploy, publish packages,
rotate secrets, or change GitHub repository settings.

## Source-Backed Comparison Policy

High-signal repositories and ecosystems are treated as design input, not as
trusted configuration to copy wholesale.

Reviewed comparison sources include:

- ECC: https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC
- OpenAI skills: https://github.com/openai/skills
- Anthropic skills: https://github.com/anthropics/skills
- Awesome Copilot: https://github.com/github/awesome-copilot
- MCP servers: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers
- Playwright MCP: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp
- Browser Use: https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use
- SuperClaude Framework: https://github.com/SuperClaude-Org/SuperClaude_Framework

Adopted patterns are mapped to the smallest Codex-native surface:

- Durable repo behavior -> `AGENTS.md`
- Reusable workflows -> skills
- Distributed workflow bundles -> plugins
- Live external context -> MCP/connectors
- Specialist review or evidence work -> subagents
- Command exceptions -> rules
- Lifecycle automation -> hooks after security review
- Push, release, deploy, external upload -> explicit approval gate

Rejected default patterns include broad cross-harness sync, enabled
authenticated connectors, prompt-injection lifecycle hooks, cookie import,
tunnels, auto-commit, auto-deploy, raw credential handling, and unreviewed large
skill catalogs.

## Official Codex Source Route

Use the current OpenAI Codex manual and official docs as source of truth for
Codex surfaces, AGENTS.md, skills, plugins, MCP, hooks, rules, permissions,
config, subagents, noninteractive mode, and Windows behavior.

Primary reference: https://developers.openai.com/codex/codex-manual.md

## Verification

Use the repository gates before claiming the setup is ready:

```bash
npm run check
npm run verify:skills:online
git diff --check
gitleaks detect --redact --no-banner --no-git --verbose
```

PowerShell dry-run:

```powershell
.\scripts\install.ps1 -All -Force -WhatIf
```

Bash or WSL dry-run:

```bash
./scripts/install.sh --all --force --dry-run
```

## Agent Instructions

For work inside this repository, read `AGENTS.md` first. Then prefer the
repository scripts and validators over ad hoc edits.

Do not add secrets, local user paths, auth files, session state, build output,
archives, generated scratch data, or dependency folders to tracked source.

Do not weaken validation, approval gates, sandbox defaults, or disabled
connector defaults to make a check pass.
